title: Create Your First Theme
excerpt: Learn how to create your first Static Website Generator theme using Astro and the Theme Starter.
content:
# Create Your First Theme

Building a theme for Static Website Generator starts with the official Theme Starter.

## Clone the Theme Starter

Clone the repository and install the dependencies.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/staticwebsitegenerator/theme-starter.git
cd swg-theme
npm install
```

## Start the Development Server

Run Astro in development mode.

```bash
npm run dev
```

The Theme Starter contains example pages, blog articles and documentation that mirror the SWG export format.

## Theme Structure

A theme consists of layouts, components and the required SWG templates.

```text
src/
├── components/
├── layouts/
├── templates/
└── styles/
```

The internal structure is completely up to the theme developer.

## Required Templates

Every theme must provide the required SWG templates.

- Homepage
- Page
- Blog Index
- Blog Category
- Blog Article
- Docs Index
- Docs Category
- Docs Article

These templates form the contract between your theme and Static Website Generator.

## Validate Your Theme

Before packaging your theme, validate it.

```bash
npm run validate
```

The validator checks whether your theme is compatible with SWG.

## Package Your Theme

When your theme is finished, create a distributable package.

```bash
npm run package
```

The generated package can be imported directly into Static Website Generator.

data.title: Validate and package your SWG theme.
